sanskrit:
यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्त्मः।
अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः॥
transliteration:
yasmāt kṣaram atīto ’ham akṣarād api cottamaḥ | 
ato ’smi loke vede ca prathitaḥ puruṣottamaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Because I transcend the perishable and am also higher than the imperishable, therefore I am proclaimed in the world and in the Veda as the Supreme Person.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names a trans-personal ground that holds both the time-bound self (kṣara) and the timeless witness (akṣara).  Psychologically this is the observing awareness that can contain every changing state and every fixed identity without being defined by either.
psychological_state: Expansion from ego-identity to meta-positional awareness; the felt sense that ‘I’ am larger than any role, mood, or life-stage.
trigger_scenario: A therapist invites a client caught in suicidal hopelessness to notice ‘the part that can observe the despair’—suddenly the despair is an object within a larger, untouched awareness.
anchor_text: Rest as the awareness that holds every thought, feeling, and role without being any of them.
